regions.text: The path from your toe to your brain and back can be twelve feet. Signals travel this loop to help you step and move your foot.
(Diagram: A person on a ladder illustrating the signal loop from toe to brain).
Walking requires almost all your muscles to work together to keep you balanced. Nerves and muscles keep you on your feet.
(Diagram: Human muscular system in a walking pose). Nerve and muscle cells depend on other cells throughout the body.
